2: // MODULE: Incremental Index
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Feature catalog: Deferred lexical-only indexing
5: // Mtime-based incremental indexing for fast re-indexing
6: // Node stdlib
7: import { createHash } from 'node:crypto';
8: import * as fs from 'fs';
9: import * as path from 'path';

12: import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';

20: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
21:    1. CONSTANTS
22: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
23: 
24: const MTIME_FAST_PATH_MS = 1000; // Skip if mtime within 1 second
25: const DEFAULT_ORPHAN_SWEEP_LIMIT = 200;
26: 
27: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
28:    2. INTERFACES
29: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
30: 
31: interface FileMetadata {
32:   path: string;
33:   mtime: number;
34:   size: number;
35:   exists: boolean;
36: }
37: 
38: interface StoredMetadata {
39:   file_path: string;
40:   canonical_file_path?: string | null;
41:   file_mtime_ms: number | null;
42:   content_hash: string | null;
43:   embedding_status: string;
44: }
45: 
46: type IndexDecision = 'skip' | 'reindex' | 'new' | 'deleted' | 'modified' | 'unknown';
47:

49:   toIndex: string[];
50:   toUpdate: string[];
51:   toSkip: string[];
52:   toDelete: string[];
53: }
54:

62: }

77:    3. MODULE STATE
78: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/

80: let db: Database.Database | null = null;
81: let canonicalPathColumnAvailable: boolean | null = null;

90: function hasCanonicalPathColumn(): boolean {

93:   if (canonicalPathColumnAvailable !== null) {
94:     return canonicalPathColumnAvailable;

124: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
125:    4. FILE METADATA
126: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
127: 
128: function getFileMetadata(filePath: string): FileMetadata {
129:   try {
130:     const stats = fs.statSync(filePath);
131:     return {
132:       path: filePath,
133:       mtime: stats.mtimeMs,
134:       size: stats.size,
135:       exists: true,
136:     };
137:   } catch {
138:     return {
139:       path: filePath,
140:       mtime: 0,
141:       size: 0,
142:       exists: false,
143:     };
144:   }
145: }
146: 
147: function getStoredMetadata(filePath: string): StoredMetadata | null {
148:   if (!db) return null;
149: 
150:   try {
151:     const canonicalPath = getCanonicalPathKey(filePath);
152: 
153:     const row = hasCanonicalPathColumn()
154:       ? (db.prepare(`
155:           SELECT file_path, canonical_file_path, file_mtime_ms, content_hash, embedding_status
156:           FROM memory_index
157:           WHERE canonical_file_path = ? OR file_path = ?
158:           ORDER BY CASE WHEN canonical_file_path = ? THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, id DESC
159:           LIMIT 1
160:         `) as Database.Statement).get(canonicalPath, filePath, canonicalPath) as StoredMetadata | undefined
161:       : (db.prepare(`
162:           SELECT file_path, file_mtime_ms, content_hash, embedding_status
163:           FROM memory_index
164:           WHERE file_path = ?
165:         `) as Database.Statement).get(filePath) as StoredMetadata | undefined;
166: 
167:     return row || null;
168:   } catch (error: unknown) {
169:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
170:     console.warn(`[incremental-index] getStoredMetadata error: ${msg}`);
171:     return null;
172:   }
173: }
174: 
175: function computeFileContentHash(filePath: string): string | null {
176:   try {
177:     const content = fs.readFileSync(filePath);
178:     return createHash('sha256').update(content).digest('hex');
179:   } catch {
180:     return null;
181:   }
182: }

184: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
185:    5. DECISION LOGIC
186: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
187: 
188: /**
189:  * 6-path decision logic for whether a file needs re-indexing.
190:  */
191: function shouldReindex(filePath: string): IndexDecision {
192:   const fileInfo = getFileMetadata(filePath);
193: 
194:   // Path 1: File doesn't exist on disk
195:   if (!fileInfo.exists) {
196:     const stored = getStoredMetadata(filePath);
197:     if (stored) return 'deleted';
198:     return 'skip';
199:   }
200: 
201:   // Path 2: No stored metadata (new file)
202:   const stored = getStoredMetadata(filePath);
203:   if (!stored) return 'new';
204: 
205:   // Path 3: No stored mtime (legacy entry, needs re-indexing)
206:   if (stored.file_mtime_ms === null) return 'reindex';
207: 
208:   // Path 4: Mtime unchanged (fast path - skip)
209:   if (Math.abs(fileInfo.mtime - stored.file_mtime_ms) < MTIME_FAST_PATH_MS) {
210:     // Still check if embedding status is pending
211:     if (stored.embedding_status === 'pending' || stored.embedding_status === 'failed') {
212:       return 'reindex';
213:     }
214: 
215:     if (stored.content_hash) {
216:       const currentContentHash = computeFileContentHash(filePath);
217:       if (currentContentHash && currentContentHash !== stored.content_hash) {
218:         return 'modified';
219:       }
220:     }
221: 
222:     return 'skip';
223:   }
224: 
225:   // Path 5: Mtime changed (file modified)
226:   return 'modified';
227: }

229: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
230:    6. MTIME MANAGEMENT
231: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
232: 
233: function updateFileMtime(filePath: string, mtimeMs: number): boolean {
234:   if (!db) return false;
235: 
236:   try {
237:     const canonicalPath = getCanonicalPathKey(filePath);
238: 
239:     const result = hasCanonicalPathColumn()
240:       ? (db.prepare(`
241:           UPDATE memory_index
242:           SET file_mtime_ms = ?
243:           WHERE canonical_file_path = ? OR file_path = ?
244:         `) as Database.Statement).run(mtimeMs, canonicalPath, filePath)
245:       : (db.prepare(`
246:           UPDATE memory_index
247:           SET file_mtime_ms = ?
248:           WHERE file_path = ?
249:         `) as Database.Statement).run(mtimeMs, filePath);
250: 
251:     return (result as { changes: number }).changes > 0;
252:   } catch (error: unknown) {
253:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
254:     console.warn(`[incremental-index] updateFileMtime error: ${msg}`);
255:     return false;
256:   }
257: }
258: 
259: function setIndexedMtime(filePath: string): boolean {
260:   const fileInfo = getFileMetadata(filePath);
261:   if (!fileInfo.exists) return false;
262:   return updateFileMtime(filePath, fileInfo.mtime);
263: }

265: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
266:    7. BATCH OPERATIONS
267: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
268: 
269: function categorizeFilesForIndexing(filePaths: string[]): CategorizedFiles {
270:   const result: CategorizedFiles = {
271:     toIndex: [],
272:     toUpdate: [],
273:     toSkip: [],
274:     toDelete: [],
275:   };
276: 
277:   for (const filePath of filePaths) {
278:     const decision = shouldReindex(filePath);
279: 
280:     switch (decision) {
281:       case 'new':
282:         result.toIndex.push(filePath);
283:         break;
284:       case 'modified':
285:       case 'reindex':
286:         result.toUpdate.push(filePath);
287:         break;
288:       case 'deleted':
289:         result.toDelete.push(filePath);
290:         break;
291:       case 'skip':
292:       default:
293:         result.toSkip.push(filePath);
294:         break;
295:     }
296:   }
297: 
298:   // Include stale indexed paths that are no longer discovered on disk.
299:   // Without this pass, removed files never enter toDelete during normal scans
300:   // Because discovery only returns files that currently exist.
301:   const staleIndexedPaths = listStaleIndexedPaths(filePaths);
302:   if (staleIndexedPaths.length > 0) {
303:     const seenDeleteKeys = new Set<string>(result.toDelete.map((filePath) => getCanonicalPathKey(filePath)));
304:     for (const stalePath of staleIndexedPaths) {
305:       const staleKey = getCanonicalPathKey(stalePath);
306:       if (!seenDeleteKeys.has(staleKey)) {
307:         result.toDelete.push(stalePath);
308:         seenDeleteKeys.add(staleKey);
309:       }
310:     }
311:   }
312: 
313:   return result;
314: }
315: 
316: function listStaleIndexedPaths(scannedFilePaths: string[]): string[] {
317:   if (!db) return [];
318: 
319:   const scannedCanonicalPaths = new Set(scannedFilePaths.map((filePath) => getCanonicalPathKey(filePath)));
320:   const stalePaths = new Set<string>();
321: 
322:   try {
323:     const rows = hasCanonicalPathColumn()
324:       ? (db.prepare(`
325:           SELECT DISTINCT file_path, canonical_file_path
326:           FROM memory_index
327:           WHERE file_path IS NOT NULL AND file_path != ''
328:         `) as Database.Statement).all() as IndexedPathRow[]
329:       : (db.prepare(`
330:           SELECT DISTINCT file_path
331:           FROM memory_index
332:           WHERE file_path IS NOT NULL AND file_path != ''
333:         `) as Database.Statement).all() as IndexedPathRow[];
334: 
335:     for (const row of rows) {
336:       if (!row || typeof row.file_path !== 'string' || row.file_path.length === 0) {
337:         continue;
338:       }
339: 
340:       const rowCanonicalPath = typeof row.canonical_file_path === 'string' && row.canonical_file_path.length > 0
341:         ? row.canonical_file_path
342:         : getCanonicalPathKey(row.file_path);
343: 
344:       if (scannedCanonicalPaths.has(rowCanonicalPath)) {
345:         continue;
346:       }
347: 
348:       const filePathExists = getFileMetadata(row.file_path).exists;
349:       const canonicalPathExists = typeof row.canonical_file_path === 'string' && row.canonical_file_path.length > 0
350:         ? getFileMetadata(row.canonical_file_path).exists
351:         : false;
352: 
353:       if (!filePathExists && !canonicalPathExists) {
354:         stalePaths.add(row.file_path);
355:       }
356:     }
357:   } catch (error: unknown) {
358:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
359:     console.warn(`[incremental-index] listStaleIndexedPaths error: ${msg}`);
360:   }
361: 
362:   return Array.from(stalePaths);
363: }
364: 
365: function listIndexedRecordIdsForDeletedPaths(filePaths: string[]): number[] {
366:   if (!db || filePaths.length === 0) return [];
367: 
368:   const ids = new Set<number>();
369:   const seenLookupKeys = new Set<string>();
370: 
371:   try {
372:     for (const filePath of filePaths) {
373:       if (typeof filePath !== 'string' || filePath.length === 0) {
374:         continue;
375:       }
376: 
377:       const canonicalPath = getCanonicalPathKey(filePath);
378:       const lookupKey = `${canonicalPath}::${filePath}`;
379:       if (seenLookupKeys.has(lookupKey)) {
380:         continue;
381:       }
382:       seenLookupKeys.add(lookupKey);
383: 
384:       const rows = hasCanonicalPathColumn()
385:         ? (db.prepare(`
386:             SELECT id, file_path, canonical_file_path
387:             FROM memory_index
388:             WHERE canonical_file_path = ? OR file_path = ?
389:           `) as Database.Statement).all(canonicalPath, filePath) as IndexedRecordRow[]
390:         : (db.prepare(`
391:             SELECT id, file_path
392:             FROM memory_index
393:             WHERE file_path = ?
394:           `) as Database.Statement).all(filePath) as IndexedRecordRow[];
395: 
396:       for (const row of rows) {
397:         if (!row || typeof row.id !== 'number') {
398:           continue;
399:         }
400: 
401:         const rowFileExists = typeof row.file_path === 'string' && row.file_path.length > 0
402:           ? getFileMetadata(row.file_path).exists
403:           : false;
404:         const rowCanonicalExists = typeof row.canonical_file_path === 'string' && row.canonical_file_path.length > 0
405:           ? getFileMetadata(row.canonical_file_path).exists
406:           : false;
407: 
408:         if (rowFileExists || rowCanonicalExists) {
409:           continue;
410:         }
411: 
412:         ids.add(row.id);
413:       }
414:     }
415:   } catch (error: unknown) {
416:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
417:     console.warn(`[incremental-index] listIndexedRecordIdsForDeletedPaths error: ${msg}`);
418:     return [];
419:   }
420: 
421:   return Array.from(ids).sort((a, b) => a - b);
422: }
423: 
424: function normalizeOrphanSweepLimit(limit?: number): number {
425:   if (typeof limit !== 'number' || !Number.isFinite(limit) || limit <= 0) {
426:     return DEFAULT_ORPHAN_SWEEP_LIMIT;
427:   }
428: 
429:   return Math.max(1, Math.floor(limit));
430: }
431: 
432: function indexedRecordIsAbsent(row: IndexedRecordRow): boolean {
433:   const rowFileExists = typeof row.file_path === 'string' && row.file_path.length > 0
434:     ? getFileMetadata(row.file_path).exists
435:     : false;
436:   const rowCanonicalExists = typeof row.canonical_file_path === 'string' && row.canonical_file_path.length > 0
437:     ? getFileMetadata(row.canonical_file_path).exists
438:     : false;
439: 
440:   return !rowFileExists && !rowCanonicalExists;
441: }
442: 
443: function sweepOrphanIndexRows(options: OrphanSweepOptions = {}): OrphanSweepResult {
444:   if (!db) {
445:     return { swept: 0, nextCursor: null, scannedRows: 0, orphanRecordIds: [] };
446:   }
447: 
448:   const limit = normalizeOrphanSweepLimit(options.limit);
449:   const cursor = typeof options.cursor === 'number' && Number.isFinite(options.cursor) && options.cursor > 0
450:     ? Math.floor(options.cursor)
451:     : 0;
452: 
453:   try {
454:     const rows = hasCanonicalPathColumn()
455:       ? (db.prepare(`
456:           SELECT id, file_path, canonical_file_path
457:           FROM memory_index
458:           WHERE id > ?
459:             AND file_path IS NOT NULL
460:             AND file_path != ''
461:           ORDER BY id ASC
462:           LIMIT ?
463:         `) as Database.Statement).all(cursor, limit) as IndexedRecordRow[]
464:       : (db.prepare(`
465:           SELECT id, file_path
466:           FROM memory_index
467:           WHERE id > ?
468:             AND file_path IS NOT NULL
469:             AND file_path != ''
470:           ORDER BY id ASC
471:           LIMIT ?
472:         `) as Database.Statement).all(cursor, limit) as IndexedRecordRow[];
473: 
474:     const orphanRecordIds: number[] = [];
475:     for (const row of rows) {
476:       if (!row || typeof row.id !== 'number') {
477:         continue;
478:       }
479:       if (indexedRecordIsAbsent(row)) {
480:         orphanRecordIds.push(row.id);
481:       }
482:     }
483: 
484:     return {
485:       swept: orphanRecordIds.length,
486:       nextCursor: rows.length === limit ? rows[rows.length - 1]?.id ?? null : null,
487:       scannedRows: rows.length,
488:       orphanRecordIds,
489:     };
490:   } catch (error: unknown) {
491:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
492:     console.warn(`[incremental-index] sweepOrphanIndexRows error: ${msg}`);
493:     return { swept: 0, nextCursor: null, scannedRows: 0, orphanRecordIds: [] };
494:   }
495: }

497: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
498:    7b. MOVE RECONCILIATION
499: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
500: 
501: interface MoveReconcileCandidate {
502:   oldPath: string;
503:   newPath: string;
504:   rowId: number;
505:   docType: string;
506: }
507: 
508: interface ReconcileMovesResult {
509:   reconciled: MoveReconcileCandidate[];
510:   filteredToDelete: string[];
511:   filteredToIndex: string[];
512: }
513: 
514: interface IncrementalChunkFingerprint {
515:   chunkId: string;
516:   chunkFingerprint: string;
517: }
518: 
519: interface MemoizedPlanningInput {
520:   componentPath: string;
521:   canonicalInput: unknown;
522:   codeHash: string;
523:   chunks?: IncrementalChunkFingerprint[];
524: }
525: 
526: interface MemoizedPlanningOptions {
527:   invalidateDependents?: boolean;
528: }
529: 
530: interface MemoizedPlanningResult {
531:   memoHits: number;
532:   memoMisses: number;
533:   chunkHits: number;
534:   chunkMisses: number;
535:   dependencyInvalidated: number;
536:   changedComponentPaths: string[];
537:   unchangedComponentPaths: string[];
538:   invalidatedComponentPaths: string[];
539: }
540: 
541: function reconcileMoves(
542:   toDelete: string[],
543:   toIndex: string[],
544: ): ReconcileMovesResult {
545:   if (!db || toDelete.length === 0 || toIndex.length === 0) {
546:     return { reconciled: [], filteredToDelete: toDelete, filteredToIndex: toIndex };
547:   }
548: 
549:   // Build a map: packet_id → [{newPath, docType, newGrandparent}] from the toIndex files.
550:   // Reads graph-metadata.json from the NEW location only — the old one may be gone after a move.
551:   const newByPacketId = new Map<string, Array<{ newPath: string; docType: string; newGrandparent: string }>>();
552:   for (const newPath of toIndex) {
553:     try {
554:       const newSpecFolder = path.dirname(newPath);
555:       const graphMetaPath = path.join(newSpecFolder, 'graph-metadata.json');
556:       if (!fs.existsSync(graphMetaPath)) continue;
557:       const raw = fs.readFileSync(graphMetaPath, 'utf-8');
558:       const meta = JSON.parse(raw) as { packet_id?: string };
559:       const packetId = meta?.packet_id;
560:       if (typeof packetId !== 'string' || packetId.length === 0) continue;
561:       const docType = path.basename(newPath, path.extname(newPath));
562:       const newGrandparent = path.dirname(newSpecFolder);
563:       if (!newByPacketId.has(packetId)) newByPacketId.set(packetId, []);
564:       newByPacketId.get(packetId)!.push({ newPath, docType, newGrandparent });
565:     } catch {
566:       // skip unreadable or non-JSON graph-metadata
567:     }
568:   }
569: 
570:   if (newByPacketId.size === 0) {
571:     return { reconciled: [], filteredToDelete: toDelete, filteredToIndex: toIndex };
572:   }
573: 
574:   const reconciledOldPaths = new Set<string>();
575:   const reconciledNewPaths = new Set<string>();
576:   const reconciled: MoveReconcileCandidate[] = [];
577: 
578:   // Iterate from the NEW side: for each new path whose folder still carries a packet_id,
579:   // find the corresponding old path in toDelete by matching grandparent directory + basename
580:   // (a sibling folder rename within the same parent). The packet_id only gates which new
581:   // paths are eligible — the actual old→new pairing is grandparent + basename plus the
582:   // DB uniqueness guards below; it does NOT match the old row by its packet_id (the old
583:   // graph-metadata.json may be gone, and memory_index has no packet_id column). To avoid
584:   // repointing across mismatched document kinds, the stored document_type is cross-checked
585:   // against the doc type derived from the new path before the in-place update.
586:   const docTypeAvailable = hasDocumentTypeColumn();
587:   const selectSql = docTypeAvailable
588:     ? `SELECT id, document_type FROM memory_index WHERE (file_path = ? OR canonical_file_path = ?) AND embedding_status != 'failed' LIMIT 2`
589:     : `SELECT id FROM memory_index WHERE (file_path = ? OR canonical_file_path = ?) AND embedding_status != 'failed' LIMIT 2`;
590:   for (const [, newCandidates] of newByPacketId) {
591:     for (const { newPath, docType, newGrandparent } of newCandidates) {
592:       if (reconciledNewPaths.has(newPath)) continue;
593: 
594:       const newBasename = path.basename(newPath);
595: 
596:       // Find toDelete paths that are sibling renames: same grandparent dir, same basename.
597:       // A sibling rename means only the immediate spec folder name changed (e.g. 012-old → 012-new).
598:       const siblingDeleted = toDelete.filter(oldPath =>
599:         !reconciledOldPaths.has(oldPath) &&
600:         path.basename(oldPath) === newBasename &&
601:         path.dirname(path.dirname(oldPath)) === newGrandparent
602:       );
603: 
604:       if (siblingDeleted.length !== 1) continue; // uniqueness required
605: 
606:       const oldPath = siblingDeleted[0];
607:       const canonicalOld = getCanonicalPathKey(oldPath);
608: 
609:       // Verify in DB: exactly one live row for this old path (uniqueness guard).
610:       const rows = db!.prepare(selectSql).all(oldPath, canonicalOld) as Array<{ id: number; document_type?: string | null }>;
611:       if (rows.length !== 1) continue;
612: 
613:       // Cross-verify document kind: the stored document_type must agree with the doc type
614:       // derived from the new path. Skip rows whose stored type diverged from the new file
615:       // (e.g. a generic 'memory' row that happens to share a basename), preventing a
616:       // mismatched repoint. Tolerant when the column is absent (legacy/test schemas).
617:       if (docTypeAvailable) {
618:         const storedDocType = rows[0].document_type ?? null;
619:         if (storedDocType !== null && storedDocType !== extractDocumentType(newPath)) continue;
620:       }
621: 
622:       const rowId = rows[0].id;
623:       const newCanonical = getCanonicalPathKey(newPath);
624:       const newSpecFolder = extractSpecFolder(newPath);
625:       const newMtime = (() => {
626:         try { return fs.statSync(newPath).mtimeMs; } catch { return null; }
627:       })();
628: 
629:       // Update the row's path in place (preserves embedding, id, history). A reconciled
630:       // move is a folder rename, so spec_folder is repointed with the same canonicalization
631:       // indexMemoryFile/migration 23 use — otherwise WHERE spec_folder=? grouping goes stale.
632:       const updated = db!.prepare(`
633:         UPDATE memory_index
634:         SET file_path = ?,
635:             canonical_file_path = ?,
636:             spec_folder = ?,
637:             file_mtime_ms = ?,
638:             updated_at = datetime('now')
639:         WHERE id = ?
640:           AND embedding_status != 'failed'
641:       `).run(newPath, newCanonical, newSpecFolder, newMtime, rowId);
642: 
643:       if ((updated as { changes: number }).changes !== 1) continue;
644: 
645:       reconciledOldPaths.add(oldPath);
646:       reconciledNewPaths.add(newPath);
647:       reconciled.push({ oldPath, newPath, rowId, docType });
648:       console.error(`[incremental-index] Move reconciled: ${newBasename} ${oldPath} → ${newPath} (row ${rowId}, embedding preserved)`);
649:     }
650:   }
651: 
652:   return {
653:     reconciled,
654:     filteredToDelete: toDelete.filter(p => !reconciledOldPaths.has(p)),
655:     filteredToIndex: toIndex.filter(p => !reconciledNewPaths.has(p)),
656:   };
657: }
658: 
659: function batchUpdateMtimes(filePaths: string[]): { updated: number; failed: number } {
660:   if (!db) return { updated: 0, failed: filePaths.length };
661: 
662:   let updated = 0;
663:   let failed = 0;
664: 
665:   const updateTx = db.transaction(() => {
666:     for (const filePath of filePaths) {
667:       if (setIndexedMtime(filePath)) {
668:         updated++;
669:       } else {
670:         failed++;
671:       }
672:     }
673:   });
674: 
675:   try {
676:     updateTx();
677:   } catch (error: unknown) {
678:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
679:     console.warn(`[incremental-index] batchUpdateMtimes error: ${msg}`);
680:   }
681: 
682:   return { updated, failed };
683: }
684: 
685: function countChunkFingerprintHits(database: Database.Database, input: MemoizedPlanningInput): { hits: number; misses: number } {
686:   if (!input.chunks || input.chunks.length === 0) {
687:     return { hits: 0, misses: 0 };
688:   }
689: 
690:   try {
691:     const stmt = database.prepare(`
692:       SELECT 1 AS hit
693:       FROM memory_index
694:       WHERE (canonical_file_path = ? OR file_path = ?)
695:         AND chunk_id = ?
696:         AND chunk_fingerprint = ?
697:       LIMIT 1
698:     `);
699:     const canonicalPath = getCanonicalPathKey(input.componentPath);
700:     let hits = 0;
701:     let misses = 0;
702:     for (const chunk of input.chunks) {
703:       const row = stmt.get(canonicalPath, input.componentPath, chunk.chunkId, chunk.chunkFingerprint) as { hit?: number } | undefined;
704:       if (row?.hit === 1) {
705:         hits++;
706:       } else {
707:         misses++;
708:       }
709:     }
710:     return { hits, misses };
711:   } catch (error: unknown) {
712:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
713:     console.warn(`[incremental-index] countChunkFingerprintHits error: ${msg}`);
714:     return { hits: 0, misses: input.chunks.length };
715:   }
716: }
717: 
718: function planMemoizedIndexing(
719:   inputs: readonly MemoizedPlanningInput[],
720:   options: MemoizedPlanningOptions = {},
721:   database: Database.Database | null = db,
722: ): MemoizedPlanningResult {
723:   const result: MemoizedPlanningResult = {
724:     memoHits: 0,
725:     memoMisses: 0,
726:     chunkHits: 0,
727:     chunkMisses: 0,
728:     dependencyInvalidated: 0,
729:     changedComponentPaths: [],
730:     unchangedComponentPaths: [],
731:     invalidatedComponentPaths: [],
732:   };
733: 
734:   if (!database) {
735:     result.changedComponentPaths = inputs.map((input) => input.componentPath);
736:     result.memoMisses = inputs.length;
737:     result.chunkMisses = inputs.reduce((count, input) => count + (input.chunks?.length ?? 0), 0);
738:     return result;
739:   }
740: 
741:   const memoStore = createMemoStore(database);
742:   const changedPaths: string[] = [];
743:   for (const input of inputs) {
744:     const inputFingerprint = canonicalFingerprint(input.canonicalInput);
745:     const memo = memoStore.getMemoRecord(input.componentPath, inputFingerprint, input.codeHash);
746:     if (memo) {
747:       result.memoHits++;
748:       result.unchangedComponentPaths.push(input.componentPath);
749:     } else {
750:       result.memoMisses++;
751:       result.changedComponentPaths.push(input.componentPath);
752:       changedPaths.push(input.componentPath);
753:     }
754: 
755:     const chunkCounts = countChunkFingerprintHits(database, input);
756:     result.chunkHits += chunkCounts.hits;
757:     result.chunkMisses += chunkCounts.misses;
758:   }
759: 
760:   if (options.invalidateDependents !== false && changedPaths.length > 0) {
761:     const invalidation = memoStore.invalidateDependents(changedPaths);
762:     result.invalidatedComponentPaths = invalidation.invalidatedPaths;
763:     result.dependencyInvalidated = invalidation.invalidatedPaths.length;
764:   }
765: 
766:   return result;
767: }

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: State Diff Reconciliation
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: import { createHash } from 'node:crypto';
5: 
6: type JsonValue = null | boolean | number | string | JsonValue[] | { [key: string]: JsonValue };
7: 
8: export type DiffActionKind = 'insert' | 'upsert' | 'replace' | 'delete';
9: 
10: export type StatediffTargetType =
11:   | 'memory_index'
12:   | 'embedding'
13:   | 'embedding_cache'
14:   | 'fts'
15:   | 'bm25'
16:   | 'graph_edge'
17:   | 'causal_edge'
18:   | 'cache'
19:   | 'child_projection'

21: 
22: export interface TargetStateRow<TPayload extends JsonValue = JsonValue> {
23:   readonly target: StatediffTargetType;
24:   readonly key: string;
25:   readonly payload: TPayload;
26:   readonly hash?: string;
27:   readonly dependencyKeys?: readonly string[];
28: }
29: 
30: export interface StatediffAction<TPayload extends JsonValue = JsonValue> {
31:   readonly action: DiffActionKind;
32:   readonly target: StatediffTargetType;
33:   readonly key: string;
34:   readonly oldStateHash: string | null;
35:   readonly newStateHash: string | null;

37:   readonly desired: TargetStateRow<TPayload> | null;
38:   readonly sourceOperation: string;
39:   readonly metadata?: Readonly<Record<string, JsonValue>>;
40: }
41: 
42: export interface PlanStatediffOptions {
43:   readonly sourceOperation: string;
44:   readonly incompletePrior?: boolean;
45:   readonly metadata?: Readonly<Record<string, JsonValue>>;
46: }
47: 
48: export interface CompositeTarget<TPayload extends JsonValue = JsonValue> {
49:   readonly parent: TargetStateRow<TPayload>;
50:   readonly children?: readonly TargetStateRow<TPayload>[];
51: }
52: 
53: export interface StatediffActionBatch<TPayload extends JsonValue = JsonValue> {
54:   readonly sourceOperation: string;
55:   readonly actions: readonly StatediffAction<TPayload>[];
56: }
57: 
58: export interface StatediffTargetSink<TPayload extends JsonValue = JsonValue> {
59:   readonly target: StatediffTargetType;
60:   apply(action: StatediffAction<TPayload>): void | Promise<void>;
61: }
62: 
63: export interface StatediffSubscriberResult {
64:   readonly name: string;
65:   readonly actionCount: number;
66:   readonly ok: boolean;
67:   readonly error?: string;
68: }
69: 
70: export interface StatediffSubscriber<TPayload extends JsonValue = JsonValue> {
71:   readonly name: string;
72:   shouldRun(actions: readonly StatediffAction<TPayload>[]): boolean;
73:   run(actions: readonly StatediffAction<TPayload>[]): void | Promise<void>;
74: }
75: 
76: function sortRows<TPayload extends JsonValue>(rows: readonly TargetStateRow<TPayload>[]): TargetStateRow<TPayload>[] {
77:   return [...rows].sort((left, right) => {
78:     const targetCompare = String(left.target).localeCompare(String(right.target));
79:     return targetCompare !== 0 ? targetCompare : left.key.localeCompare(right.key);
80:   });
81: }
82: 
83: function stableJson(value: JsonValue): string {
84:   if (value === null || typeof value !== 'object') {
85:     return JSON.stringify(value);
86:   }
87: 
88:   if (Array.isArray(value)) {
89:     return `[${value.map((item) => stableJson(item)).join(',')}]`;
90:   }
91: 
92:   const entries = Object.entries(value).sort(([left], [right]) => left.localeCompare(right));
93:   return `{${entries.map(([key, item]) => `${JSON.stringify(key)}:${stableJson(item)}`).join(',')}}`;
94: }
95: 
96: function stateHash(row: TargetStateRow | null | undefined): string | null {
97:   if (!row) {
98:     return null;
99:   }
100:   if (row.hash) {
101:     return row.hash;
102:   }
103:   return createHash('sha256').update(stableJson(row.payload)).digest('hex');
104: }
105: 
106: function rowId(row: Pick<TargetStateRow, 'target' | 'key'>): string {
107:   return `${row.target}\u0000${row.key}`;
108: }
109: 
110: function createAction<TPayload extends JsonValue>(
111:   action: DiffActionKind,
112:   target: StatediffTargetType,
113:   key: string,
114:   prior: TargetStateRow<TPayload> | null,
115:   desired: TargetStateRow<TPayload> | null,
116:   options: PlanStatediffOptions,
117: ): StatediffAction<TPayload> {
118:   return {
119:     action,
120:     target,
121:     key,
122:     oldStateHash: stateHash(prior),
123:     newStateHash: stateHash(desired),
124:     prior,
125:     desired,
126:     sourceOperation: options.sourceOperation,
127:     ...(options.metadata ? { metadata: options.metadata } : {}),
128:   };
129: }
130: 
131: /** Computes explicit target actions from caller-provided desired and prior rows. */
132: export function planStatediff<TPayload extends JsonValue>(
133:   desiredRows: readonly TargetStateRow<TPayload>[],
134:   priorRows: readonly TargetStateRow<TPayload>[],
135:   options: PlanStatediffOptions,
136: ): StatediffAction<TPayload>[] {
137:   const desiredById = new Map(sortRows(desiredRows).map((row) => [rowId(row), row]));
138:   const priorById = new Map(sortRows(priorRows).map((row) => [rowId(row), row]));
139:   const ids = [...new Set([...desiredById.keys(), ...priorById.keys()])].sort();
140:   const actions: StatediffAction<TPayload>[] = [];
141: 
142:   for (const id of ids) {
143:     const desired = desiredById.get(id) ?? null;
144:     const prior = priorById.get(id) ?? null;
145:     const reference = desired ?? prior;
146:     if (!reference) {
147:       continue;
148:     }
149: 
150:     if (desired && !prior) {
151:       actions.push(createAction(options.incompletePrior ? 'upsert' : 'insert', reference.target, reference.key, null, desired, options));
152:       continue;
153:     }
154: 
155:     if (!desired && prior) {
156:       actions.push(createAction('delete', reference.target, reference.key, prior, null, options));
157:       continue;
158:     }
159: 
160:     if (desired && prior && stateHash(desired) !== stateHash(prior)) {
161:       actions.push(createAction(options.incompletePrior ? 'upsert' : 'replace', reference.target, reference.key, prior, desired, options));
162:     }
163:   }
164: 
165:   return actions;
166: }

168: /** Flattens a parent target plus child projections into deterministic row order. */
169: export function flattenCompositeTarget<TPayload extends JsonValue>(
170:   composite: CompositeTarget<TPayload>,
171: ): TargetStateRow<TPayload>[] {
172:   return sortRows([composite.parent, ...(composite.children ?? [])]);
173: }
174: 
175: /** Creates a single action for existing imperative write paths that already chose truth. */
176: export function createStatediffAction<TPayload extends JsonValue>(
177:   action: DiffActionKind,
178:   params: {
179:     readonly target: StatediffTargetType;
180:     readonly key: string;
181:     readonly sourceOperation: string;
182:     readonly prior?: TargetStateRow<TPayload> | null;
183:     readonly desired?: TargetStateRow<TPayload> | null;
184:     readonly oldStateHash?: string | null;
185:     readonly newStateHash?: string | null;
186:     readonly metadata?: Readonly<Record<string, JsonValue>>;
187:   },
188: ): StatediffAction<TPayload> {
189:   const prior = params.prior ?? null;
190:   const desired = params.desired ?? null;
191:   return {
192:     action,
193:     target: params.target,
194:     key: params.key,
195:     oldStateHash: params.oldStateHash ?? stateHash(prior),
196:     newStateHash: params.newStateHash ?? stateHash(desired),
197:     prior,
198:     desired,
199:     sourceOperation: params.sourceOperation,
200:     ...(params.metadata ? { metadata: params.metadata } : {}),
201:   };
202: }
203: 
204: /** Applies actions through target sinks in deterministic order. */
205: export async function applyStatediffActions<TPayload extends JsonValue>(
206:   actions: readonly StatediffAction<TPayload>[],
207:   sinks: readonly StatediffTargetSink<TPayload>[],
208: ): Promise<StatediffActionBatch<TPayload>> {
209:   const sinkByTarget = new Map(sinks.map((sink) => [sink.target, sink]));
210:   const ordered = [...actions].sort((left, right) => {
211:     const targetCompare = String(left.target).localeCompare(String(right.target));
212:     return targetCompare !== 0 ? targetCompare : left.key.localeCompare(right.key);
213:   });
214: 
215:   for (const action of ordered) {
216:     const sink = sinkByTarget.get(action.target);
217:     if (!sink) {
218:       throw new Error(`No statediff sink registered for target "${action.target}"`);
219:     }
220:     await sink.apply(action);
221:   }
222: 
223:   return {
224:     sourceOperation: ordered[0]?.sourceOperation ?? 'unknown',
225:     actions: ordered,
226:   };
227: }
228: 
229: /** Runs action subscribers after durable sinks have applied. */
230: export async function notifyStatediffSubscribers<TPayload extends JsonValue>(
231:   actions: readonly StatediffAction<TPayload>[],
232:   subscribers: readonly StatediffSubscriber<TPayload>[],
233: ): Promise<StatediffSubscriberResult[]> {
234:   const reports: StatediffSubscriberResult[] = [];
235:   for (const subscriber of subscribers) {
236:     if (!subscriber.shouldRun(actions)) {
237:       continue;
238:     }
239:     try {
240:       await subscriber.run(actions);
241:       reports.push({ name: subscriber.name, actionCount: actions.length, ok: true });
242:     } catch (error: unknown) {
243:       const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
244:       reports.push({ name: subscriber.name, actionCount: actions.length, ok: false, error: message });
245:     }
246:   }
247:   return reports;
248: }

2: // MODULE: Mutation Ledger

4: // Feature catalog: Transaction wrappers on mutation handlers
5: // Append-only audit trail for all memory mutations
6: // SQLite triggers enforce immutability (no UPDATE/DELETE on ledger)
7: import { createHash } from 'crypto';
8: import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';

11: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

17: interface MutationLedgerEntry {

106:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS mutation_ledger (

108:     timestamp TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%fZ', 'now')),
109:     mutation_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(mutation_type IN ('create','update','delete','merge','archive','restore','reindex')),
110:     reason TEXT NOT NULL,
111:     prior_hash TEXT,
112:     new_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
113:     linked_memory_ids TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
114:     decision_meta TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
115:     actor TEXT NOT NULL,
116:     session_id TEXT
117:   )
118: `;

120: const LEDGER_INDEX_SQL = `
121:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ledger_type ON mutation_ledger(mutation_type);
122:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ledger_actor ON mutation_ledger(actor);
123:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ledger_timestamp ON mutation_ledger(timestamp);
124:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ledger_session ON mutation_ledger(session_id);
125:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ledger_memory_type_created_at ON mutation_ledger(

127:     mutation_type,
128:     timestamp
129:   )

132: const LEDGER_TRIGGER_SQL = `
133:   CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS prevent_ledger_update BEFORE UPDATE ON mutation_ledger
134:   BEGIN SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'mutation_ledger is append-only'); END;
135:   CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS prevent_ledger_delete BEFORE DELETE ON mutation_ledger

141: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/

143: function initLedger(db: Database.Database): void {

161: function appendEntry(db: Database.Database, entry: AppendEntryInput): MutationLedgerEntry {
162:   const linkedJson = JSON.stringify(entry.linked_memory_ids);
163:   const metaJson = JSON.stringify(entry.decision_meta);

176:     metaJson,
177:     entry.actor,

179:   );

181:   const id = (result as { lastInsertRowid: number | bigint }).lastInsertRowid as number;

183:   const row = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM mutation_ledger WHERE id = ?').get(id) as MutationLedgerEntry;
184:   return row;
185: }
186: 
187: function buildAutomatedMutationEventKey(input: AutomatedMutationAuditInput): string {

195: function getAutomatedMutationAuditEntry(db: Database.Database, eventKey: string): MutationLedgerEntry | null {
196:   const row = db.prepare(`
197:     SELECT *
198:     FROM mutation_ledger
199:     WHERE json_extract(decision_meta, '$.eventKey') = ?
200:     ORDER BY id ASC
201:     LIMIT 1
202:   `).get(eventKey) as MutationLedgerEntry | undefined;
203:   return row ?? null;

205: 
206: function appendAutomatedMutationAudit(
207:   db: Database.Database,
208:   input: AutomatedMutationAuditInput,
209: ): AutomatedMutationAuditResult {
210:   initLedger(db);
211:   const eventKey = buildAutomatedMutationEventKey(input);
212:   const existing = getAutomatedMutationAuditEntry(db, eventKey);
213:   if (existing) {
214:     return { appended: false, eventKey, entry: existing };
215:   }
216: 
217:   const entry = appendEntry(db, {
218:     mutation_type: input.mutation_type ?? 'update',
219:     reason: input.reason,
220:     prior_hash: null,
221:     new_hash: eventKey,
222:     linked_memory_ids: input.linked_memory_ids ?? [],
223:     decision_meta: {
224:       ...(input.decision_meta ?? {}),
225:       eventKey,
226:       sourceKind: input.source_kind,
227:       automated: true,
228:     },
229:     actor: input.actor,
230:     session_id: input.session_id ?? null,
231:   });
232: 
233:   return { appended: true, eventKey, entry };
234: }
235: 
236: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
237:    6. QUERY ENTRIES
238: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
239: 
240: function getEntries(db: Database.Database, opts: GetEntriesOptions = {}): MutationLedgerEntry[] {
241:   const conditions: string[] = [];
242:   const params: Array<string | number> = [];
243: 
244:   if (opts.mutation_type) {
245:     conditions.push('mutation_type = ?');
246:     params.push(opts.mutation_type);
247:   }
248:   if (opts.actor) {
249:     conditions.push('actor = ?');
250:     params.push(opts.actor);
251:   }
252:   if (opts.session_id) {
253:     conditions.push('session_id = ?');
254:     params.push(opts.session_id);
255:   }
256:   if (opts.since) {
257:     conditions.push('timestamp >= ?');
258:     params.push(opts.since);
259:   }
260:   if (opts.until) {
261:     conditions.push('timestamp <= ?');
262:     params.push(opts.until);
263:   }
264: 
265:   const where = conditions.length > 0 ? `WHERE ${conditions.join(' AND ')}` : '';
266:   // String interpolation in LIMIT/OFFSET is safe here because both values
267:   // Are coerced to non-negative integers via Math.floor + Math.max before use —
268:   // Math.floor guarantees no decimal component, Math.max(1,…)/Math.max(0,…)
269:   // Guarantees no negative value. No user-supplied string reaches the SQL directly.
270:   const limit = opts.limit ? `LIMIT ${Math.max(1, Math.floor(opts.limit))}` : '';
271:   // OFFSET without LIMIT is invalid SQLite syntax.
272:   // When offset is set but limit is not, emit LIMIT -1 (all rows) before OFFSET.
273:   const offset = opts.offset ? `OFFSET ${Math.max(0, Math.floor(opts.offset))}` : '';
274:   const effectiveLimit = !limit && offset ? 'LIMIT -1' : limit;
275: 
276:   // String interpolation constructs IN(?,?,?) placeholder list only —
277:   // All user values are parameterized. Accepted exception per audit H-08.
278:   const sql = `SELECT * FROM mutation_ledger ${where} ORDER BY id ASC ${effectiveLimit} ${offset}`;
279:   return db.prepare(sql).all(...params) as MutationLedgerEntry[];
280: }
281: 
282: function getEntryCount(db: Database.Database): number {
283:   const row = db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM mutation_ledger').get() as { count: number };
284:   return row.count;
285: }
286: 
287: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
288:    7. LINKED ENTRIES LOOKUP
289: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
290: 
291: function getLinkedEntries(db: Database.Database, memoryId: number): MutationLedgerEntry[] {
292:   // SQLite json_each can expand JSON arrays for matching
293:   const sql = `
294:     SELECT ml.* FROM mutation_ledger ml, json_each(ml.linked_memory_ids) je
295:     WHERE je.value = ?
296:     ORDER BY ml.id ASC
297:   `;
298:   return db.prepare(sql).all(memoryId) as MutationLedgerEntry[];
299: }
300: 
301: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
302:    8. APPEND-ONLY VERIFICATION
303: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
304: 
305: /**
306:  * Verify that append-only triggers exist on the mutation_ledger table.
307:  * Returns true if both UPDATE and DELETE triggers are present.
308:  */
309: function verifyAppendOnly(db: Database.Database): boolean {
310:   const triggers = db.prepare(
311:     "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type = 'trigger' AND tbl_name = 'mutation_ledger'"
312:   ).all() as Array<{ name: string }>;
313: 
314:   const names = triggers.map(t => t.name);
315:   return names.includes('prevent_ledger_update') && names.includes('prevent_ledger_delete');
316: }
317: 
318: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
319:    9. DIVERGENCE RETRY + ESCALATION HOOKS
320: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
321: 
322: const DEFAULT_DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_MAX_RETRIES = 3;
323: const DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_REASON = 'alias_divergence_auto_reconcile';
324: const DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_ESCALATION_REASON = 'alias_divergence_auto_reconcile_escalated';
325: const DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_ACTOR = 'memory-index-scan';
326: 
327: function normalizeMaxRetries(maxRetries?: number): number {
328:   if (!Number.isFinite(maxRetries) || (maxRetries ?? 0) < 1) {
329:     return DEFAULT_DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_MAX_RETRIES;
330:   }
331:   return Math.max(1, Math.floor(maxRetries as number));
332: }
333: 
334: function normalizePath(value: string): string {
335:   return value.trim().replace(/\\/g, '/');
336: }
337: 
338: function normalizeVariants(variants?: string[]): string[] {
339:   if (!Array.isArray(variants)) {
340:     return [];
341:   }
342: 
343:   const unique = new Set<string>();
344:   for (const variant of variants) {
345:     if (typeof variant !== 'string' || variant.trim().length === 0) {
346:       continue;
347:     }
348:     unique.add(normalizePath(variant));
349:   }
350:   return Array.from(unique).sort((a, b) => a.localeCompare(b));
351: }
352: 
353: function getDivergenceReconcileAttemptCount(db: Database.Database, normalizedPath: string): number {
354:   const targetPath = normalizePath(normalizedPath);
355:   if (targetPath.length === 0) {
356:     return 0;
357:   }
358: 
359:   // Use COUNT(*) with json_extract to filter by path in SQL rather than scanning
360:   // All decision_meta rows in application code (O(1) vs O(n) full-table scan).
361:   const row = db.prepare(`
362:     SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt
363:     FROM mutation_ledger
364:     WHERE mutation_type = 'reindex'
365:       AND reason = ?
366:       AND json_extract(decision_meta, '$.normalizedPath') = ?
367:   `).get(DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_REASON, targetPath) as { cnt: number } | undefined;
368: 
369:   return row?.cnt ?? 0;
370: }
371: 
372: function hasDivergenceEscalationEntry(db: Database.Database, normalizedPath: string): boolean {
373:   const targetPath = normalizePath(normalizedPath);
374:   if (targetPath.length === 0) {
375:     return false;
376:   }
377: 
378:   // Use COUNT(*) with json_extract to avoid O(n) full-table scan in application code.
379:   const row = db.prepare(`
380:     SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt
381:     FROM mutation_ledger
382:     WHERE mutation_type = 'reindex'
383:       AND reason = ?
384:       AND json_extract(decision_meta, '$.normalizedPath') = ?
385:   `).get(DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_ESCALATION_REASON, targetPath) as { cnt: number } | undefined;
386: 
387:   return (row?.cnt ?? 0) > 0;
388: }
389: 
390: function buildDivergenceReconcilePolicy(
391:   normalizedPath: string,
392:   attemptsSoFar: number,
393:   maxRetries: number = DEFAULT_DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_MAX_RETRIES
394: ): DivergenceReconcilePolicy {
395:   const boundedMaxRetries = normalizeMaxRetries(maxRetries);
396:   const safeAttempts = Math.max(0, Math.floor(attemptsSoFar));
397:   const nextAttempt = safeAttempts + 1;
398:   const shouldRetry = safeAttempts < boundedMaxRetries;
399: 
400:   return {
401:     normalizedPath: normalizePath(normalizedPath),
402:     attemptsSoFar: safeAttempts,
403:     nextAttempt,
404:     maxRetries: boundedMaxRetries,
405:     shouldRetry,
406:     exhausted: !shouldRetry,
407:   };
408: }
409: 
410: function buildDivergenceEscalationPayload(
411:   policy: DivergenceReconcilePolicy,
412:   variants: string[]
413: ): DivergenceEscalationPayload {
414:   return {
415:     code: 'E_DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_RETRY_EXHAUSTED',
416:     normalizedPath: policy.normalizedPath,
417:     attempts: policy.attemptsSoFar,
418:     maxRetries: policy.maxRetries,
419:     recommendation: 'manual_triage_required',
420:     reason: `Auto-reconcile exhausted after ${policy.maxRetries} attempt(s)`,
421:     variants: normalizeVariants(variants),
422:   };
423: }
424: 
425: function recordDivergenceReconcileHook(
426:   db: Database.Database,
427:   input: RecordDivergenceReconcileInput
428: ): RecordDivergenceReconcileResult {
429:   initLedger(db);
430: 
431:   const normalizedPath = normalizePath(input.normalizedPath);
432:   if (normalizedPath.length === 0) {
433:     throw new Error('normalizedPath is required for divergence reconciliation');
434:   }
435: 
436:   const maxRetries = normalizeMaxRetries(input.maxRetries);
437:   const actor = input.actor ?? DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_ACTOR;
438:   const variants = normalizeVariants(input.variants);
439: 
440:   return runInTransaction(db, () => {
441:     const attemptsSoFar = getDivergenceReconcileAttemptCount(db, normalizedPath);
442:     const policy = buildDivergenceReconcilePolicy(normalizedPath, attemptsSoFar, maxRetries);
443: 
444:     if (policy.shouldRetry) {
445:       const retryEntry = appendEntry(db, {
446:         mutation_type: 'reindex',
447:         reason: DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_REASON,
448:         prior_hash: null,
449:         new_hash: computeHash(`${normalizedPath}|attempt:${policy.nextAttempt}|max:${policy.maxRetries}`),
450:         linked_memory_ids: [],
451:         decision_meta: {
452:           normalizedPath,
453:           attempt: policy.nextAttempt,
454:           maxRetries: policy.maxRetries,
455:           boundedRetry: true,
456:           status: 'retry_scheduled',
457:           variants,
458:         },
459:         actor,
460:         session_id: input.session_id ?? null,
461:       });
462: 
463:       return {
464:         policy,
465:         retryEntryId: retryEntry.id,
466:         escalationEntryId: null,
467:         escalation: null,
468:       };
469:     }
470: 
471:     const escalation = buildDivergenceEscalationPayload(policy, variants);
472:     if (hasDivergenceEscalationEntry(db, normalizedPath)) {
473:       return {
474:         policy,
475:         retryEntryId: null,
476:         escalationEntryId: null,
477:         escalation,
478:       };
479:     }
480: 
481:     const escalationEntry = appendEntry(db, {
482:       mutation_type: 'reindex',
483:       reason: DIVERGENCE_RECONCILE_ESCALATION_REASON,
484:       prior_hash: null,
485:       new_hash: computeHash(`${normalizedPath}|escalated|attempts:${policy.attemptsSoFar}|max:${policy.maxRetries}`),
486:       linked_memory_ids: [],
487:       decision_meta: {
488:         normalizedPath,
489:         attempts: policy.attemptsSoFar,
490:         maxRetries: policy.maxRetries,
491:         boundedRetry: true,
492:         status: 'escalated',
493:         escalation,
494:       },
495:       actor,
496:       session_id: input.session_id ?? null,
497:     });
498: 
499:     return {
500:       policy,
501:       retryEntryId: null,
502:       escalationEntryId: escalationEntry.id,
503:       escalation,
504:     };
505:   });
506: }
507: 
508: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
509:    10. EXPORTS
510: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
511: 
512: export {
513:   LEDGER_SCHEMA_SQL,
514:   LEDGER_INDEX_SQL,
515:   LEDGER_TRIGGER_SQL,

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Idempotency Receipts
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: import { createHash } from 'node:crypto';
5: import type BetterSqlite3 from 'better-sqlite3';
6: 
7: import type { MCPResponse } from '../../handlers/types.js';
8: 
9: export type IdempotencyOperation = 'memory_save' | 'memory_update';
10: 
11: export interface IdempotencyReceiptInput {
12:   operation: IdempotencyOperation;
13:   contentHash: string | null;
14:   requestFingerprint: Record<string, unknown>;
15:   payload: Record<string, unknown>;
16: }
17: 
18: export interface IdempotencyReceiptKey {
19:   operation: IdempotencyOperation;
20:   receiptKey: string;
21:   contentHash: string | null;
22:   requestFingerprintHash: string;
23:   payloadHash: string;
24: }
25: 
26: export type IdempotencyLookupResult =
27:   | { status: 'miss'; key: IdempotencyReceiptKey }
28:   | { status: 'replay'; key: IdempotencyReceiptKey; response: MCPResponse }
29:   | { status: 'conflict'; key: IdempotencyReceiptKey; storedPayloadHash: string };
30: 
31: interface ReceiptRow {
32:   payload_hash: string;
33:   response_payload: string;
34: }
35: 
36: const CLIENT_TOKEN_KEYS = new Set([
37:   'idempotencykey',
38:   'idempotency_key',
39:   'idempotencytoken',
40:   'idempotency_token',
41:   'clientidempotencykey',
42:   'client_idempotency_key',
43:   'clientidempotencytoken',
44:   'client_idempotency_token',
45: ]);
46: 
47: export function isMemoryIdempotencyEnabled(): boolean {
48:   const raw = process.env.SPECKIT_MEMORY_IDEMPOTENCY?.trim().toLowerCase();
49:   return raw === '1' || raw === 'true' || raw === 'yes' || raw === 'on';
50: }
51: 
52: function normalizeForHash(value: unknown): unknown {
53:   if (Array.isArray(value)) {
54:     return value.map(normalizeForHash);
55:   }
56:   if (!value || typeof value !== 'object') {
57:     return value;
58:   }
59: 
60:   const record = value as Record<string, unknown>;
61:   const normalized: Record<string, unknown> = {};
62:   for (const key of Object.keys(record).sort()) {
63:     if (CLIENT_TOKEN_KEYS.has(key.toLowerCase())) {
64:       continue;
65:     }
66:     const normalizedValue = normalizeForHash(record[key]);
67:     if (normalizedValue !== undefined) {
68:       normalized[key] = normalizedValue;
69:     }
70:   }
71:   return normalized;
72: }
73: 
74: function hashJson(value: unknown): string {
75:   return createHash('sha256')
76:     .update(JSON.stringify(normalizeForHash(value)))
77:     .digest('hex');
78: }
79: 
80: export function deriveIdempotencyReceiptKey(input: IdempotencyReceiptInput): IdempotencyReceiptKey {
81:   const requestFingerprintHash = hashJson(input.requestFingerprint);
82:   const payloadHash = hashJson(input.payload);
83:   const material = {
84:     operation: input.operation,
85:     contentHash: input.contentHash ?? null,
86:     requestFingerprintHash,
87:   };
88:   return {
89:     operation: input.operation,
90:     receiptKey: hashJson(material),
91:     contentHash: input.contentHash ?? null,
92:     requestFingerprintHash,
93:     payloadHash,
94:   };
95: }
96: 
97: function addReplayMarker(response: MCPResponse): MCPResponse {
98:   const cloned = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(response)) as MCPResponse;
99:   const first = cloned.content?.[0];
100:   if (!first || first.type !== 'text' || typeof first.text !== 'string') {
101:     return cloned;
102:   }
103: 
104:   try {
105:     const envelope = JSON.parse(first.text) as Record<string, unknown>;
106:     const data = envelope.data && typeof envelope.data === 'object' && !Array.isArray(envelope.data)
107:       ? envelope.data as Record<string, unknown>
108:       : {};
109:     data.replayed = true;
110:     envelope.data = data;
111:     const hints = Array.isArray(envelope.hints) ? envelope.hints : [];
112:     envelope.hints = ['Replayed prior idempotent write result', ...hints];
113:     const meta = envelope.meta && typeof envelope.meta === 'object' && !Array.isArray(envelope.meta)
114:       ? envelope.meta as Record<string, unknown>
115:       : {};
116:     meta.replayed = true;
117:     envelope.meta = meta;
118:     first.text = JSON.stringify(envelope, null, 2);
119:   } catch {
120:     return cloned;
121:   }
122: 
123:   return cloned;
124: }
125: 
126: export function lookupIdempotencyReceipt(
127:   database: BetterSqlite3.Database,
128:   input: IdempotencyReceiptInput,
129: ): IdempotencyLookupResult {
130:   const key = deriveIdempotencyReceiptKey(input);
131:   const row = database.prepare(`
132:     SELECT payload_hash, response_payload
133:     FROM memory_idempotency_receipts
134:     WHERE receipt_key = ?
135:   `).get(key.receiptKey) as ReceiptRow | undefined;
136: 
137:   if (!row) {
138:     return { status: 'miss', key };
139:   }
140:   if (row.payload_hash !== key.payloadHash) {
141:     return { status: 'conflict', key, storedPayloadHash: row.payload_hash };
142:   }
143:   return {
144:     status: 'replay',
145:     key,
146:     response: addReplayMarker(JSON.parse(row.response_payload) as MCPResponse),
147:   };
148: }
149: 
150: export function storeIdempotencyReceipt(
151:   database: BetterSqlite3.Database,
152:   key: IdempotencyReceiptKey,
153:   response: MCPResponse,
154:   memoryId?: number | null,
155: ): void {
156:   database.prepare(`
157:     INSERT INTO memory_idempotency_receipts (
158:       receipt_key,
159:       operation,
160:       content_hash,
161:       request_fingerprint,
162:       payload_hash,
163:       response_payload,
164:       memory_id,
165:       updated_at
166:     ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))
167:     ON CONFLICT(receipt_key) DO UPDATE SET
168:       response_payload = excluded.response_payload,
169:       memory_id = excluded.memory_id,
170:       updated_at = datetime('now')
171:   `).run(
172:     key.receiptKey,
173:     key.operation,
174:     key.contentHash,
175:     key.requestFingerprintHash,
176:     key.payloadHash,
177:     JSON.stringify(response),
178:     memoryId ?? null,
179:   );
180: }
181: 
182: export function extractMemoryIdFromResponse(response: MCPResponse): number | null {
183:   const first = response.content?.[0];
184:   if (!first || first.type !== 'text' || typeof first.text !== 'string') {
185:     return null;
186:   }
187:   try {
188:     const envelope = JSON.parse(first.text) as { data?: { id?: unknown; updated?: unknown } };
189:     const id = envelope.data?.id ?? envelope.data?.updated;
190:     return typeof id === 'number' && Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
191:   } catch {
192:     return null;
193:   }
194: }

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Near Duplicate Advisory
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: import type BetterSqlite3 from 'better-sqlite3';
5: 
6: import * as vectorIndex from '../search/vector-index.js';
7: import type { MemoryScopeMatch } from '../../handlers/save/types.js';

9: import { isMemoryIdempotencyEnabled } from './idempotency-receipts.js';
10: 
11: export interface NearDuplicateHint {
12:   id: number;
13:   similarity: number;
14:   threshold: number;
15: }
16: 
17: interface CandidateRow {
18:   id: number;
19:   content_hash: string | null;
20:   tenant_id: string | null;
21:   user_id: string | null;
22:   agent_id: string | null;
23:   session_id: string | null;
24:   updated_at: string | null;
25:   last_dedup_checked_at: string | null;
26:   near_duplicate_of: string | null;
27: }
28: 
29: function normalizeScopeValue(value: unknown): string | null {
30:   return typeof value === 'string' && value.length > 0 ? value : null;
31: }
32: 
33: function scopeMatches(row: CandidateRow, scope: MemoryScopeMatch): boolean {
34:   return normalizeScopeValue(row.tenant_id) === normalizeScopeValue(scope.tenantId)
35:     && normalizeScopeValue(row.user_id) === normalizeScopeValue(scope.userId)
36:     && normalizeScopeValue(row.agent_id) === normalizeScopeValue(scope.agentId)
37:     && normalizeScopeValue(row.session_id) === normalizeScopeValue(scope.sessionId);
38: }
39: 
40: export function parseNearDuplicateHint(raw: unknown): NearDuplicateHint | null {
41:   if (typeof raw !== 'string' || raw.length === 0) {
42:     return null;
43:   }
44:   try {
45:     const parsed = JSON.parse(raw) as Partial<NearDuplicateHint>;
46:     if (
47:       typeof parsed.id === 'number'
48:       && Number.isInteger(parsed.id)
49:       && parsed.id > 0
50:       && typeof parsed.similarity === 'number'
51:       && Number.isFinite(parsed.similarity)
52:       && typeof parsed.threshold === 'number'
53:       && Number.isFinite(parsed.threshold)
54:     ) {
55:       return { id: parsed.id, similarity: parsed.similarity, threshold: parsed.threshold };
56:     }
57:   } catch {
58:     return null;
59:   }
60:   return null;
61: }
62: 
63: export function shouldSkipNearDuplicateCheck(database: BetterSqlite3.Database, memoryId: number): boolean {
64:   const row = database.prepare(`
65:     SELECT updated_at, last_dedup_checked_at
66:     FROM memory_index
67:     WHERE id = ?
68:   `).get(memoryId) as Pick<CandidateRow, 'updated_at' | 'last_dedup_checked_at'> | undefined;
69:   if (!row?.last_dedup_checked_at) {
70:     return false;
71:   }
72:   if (!row.updated_at) {
73:     return true;
74:   }
75:   return new Date(row.updated_at).getTime() <= new Date(row.last_dedup_checked_at).getTime();
76: }
77: 
78: export function clearNearDuplicateCheck(database: BetterSqlite3.Database, memoryId: number): void {
79:   database.prepare(`
80:     UPDATE memory_index
81:     SET near_duplicate_of = NULL,
82:         last_dedup_checked_at = NULL
83:     WHERE id = ?
84:   `).run(memoryId);
85: }
86: 
87: export function recordNearDuplicateCheck(args: {
88:   database: BetterSqlite3.Database;
89:   memoryId: number;
90:   specFolder: string;
91:   contentHash: string | null;
92:   embedding: Float32Array | null;
93:   scope?: MemoryScopeMatch;
94: }): NearDuplicateHint | null {
95:   if (!isMemoryIdempotencyEnabled() || !args.embedding) {
96:     return null;
97:   }
98:   if (shouldSkipNearDuplicateCheck(args.database, args.memoryId)) {
99:     const row = args.database.prepare('SELECT near_duplicate_of FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?')
100:       .get(args.memoryId) as { near_duplicate_of: string | null } | undefined;
101:     return parseNearDuplicateHint(row?.near_duplicate_of);
102:   }
103: 
104:   const candidates = vectorIndex.vectorSearch(args.embedding, {
105:     limit: 8,
106:     specFolder: args.specFolder,
107:     minSimilarity: Math.round(NEAR_DUPLICATE_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD * 100),
108:     includeConstitutional: false,
109:   }) as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
110:   const ids = candidates
111:     .map((candidate) => candidate.id)
112:     .filter((id): id is number => typeof id === 'number' && Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 && id !== args.memoryId);
113:   const rows = ids.length > 0
114:     ? args.database.prepare(`
115:         SELECT id, content_hash, tenant_id, user_id, agent_id, session_id, updated_at,
116:                last_dedup_checked_at, near_duplicate_of
117:         FROM memory_index
118:         WHERE id IN (${ids.map(() => '?').join(',')})
119:       `).all(...ids) as CandidateRow[]
120:     : [];
121:   const rowsById = new Map(rows.map((row) => [row.id, row]));
122: 
123:   let hint: NearDuplicateHint | null = null;
124:   for (const candidate of candidates) {
125:     const id = typeof candidate.id === 'number' ? candidate.id : null;
126:     if (id == null || id === args.memoryId) {
127:       continue;
128:     }
129:     const row = rowsById.get(id);
130:     if (!row || !scopeMatches(row, args.scope ?? {})) {
131:       continue;
132:     }
133:     if (args.contentHash && row.content_hash === args.contentHash) {
134:       continue;
135:     }
136:     const rawSimilarity = typeof candidate.similarity === 'number' ? candidate.similarity : 0;
137:     const similarity = rawSimilarity > 1 ? rawSimilarity / 100 : rawSimilarity;
138:     if (similarity >= NEAR_DUPLICATE_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD) {
139:       hint = { id, similarity, threshold: NEAR_DUPLICATE_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD };
140:       break;
141:     }
142:   }
143: 
144:   args.database.prepare(`
145:     UPDATE memory_index
146:     SET near_duplicate_of = ?,
147:         last_dedup_checked_at = datetime('now')
148:     WHERE id = ?
149:   `).run(hint ? JSON.stringify(hint) : null, args.memoryId);
150:   return hint;
151: }

64: function finiteNumber(value: unknown): number | null {

66:   if (typeof value === 'string' && value.trim().length > 0) {
67:     const parsed = Number(value);

74:   if (!value || typeof value !== 'object' || Array.isArray(value)) return {};

76:   for (const [key, raw] of Object.entries(value as Record<string, unknown>)) {
77:     const score = finiteNumber(raw);

183:     memory_retention_sweep: { ...maintenanceRuns.memory_retention_sweep, counts: { ...maintenanceRuns.memory_retention_sweep.counts } },

187: export function findInlineConflictWarnings(
188:   results: Array<Record<string, unknown>>,
189:   dbGetter: () => Database.Database,

191:   const byId = new Map<string, Record<string, unknown>>();
192:   for (const result of results) {

203:   }

213:         AND target_id IN (${placeholders})

218:     for (const row of rows) {

220:       const target = byId.get(String(row.target_id));
221:       if (!source || !target) continue;
222:       const key = [row.relation, row.source_id, row.target_id].join(':');

224:       seen.add(key);

226:         resolveDocumentAnchor(source),
227:         resolveDocumentAnchor(target),

229:       warnings.push({
230:         type: 'verify_before_applying',
231:         relation: row.relation,
232:         documents,
233:         message: `Returned memories are linked by ${row.relation}; verify before applying either document.`,
234:       });
235:     }
236:     return warnings;
237:   } catch {
238:     return [];
239:   }
240: }

248: };
